authoritative_sources.title: Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's is the U.S. advertising agency trade association; its public code is authoritative for agency conduct, confidentiality, proprietary information, and ethical handling that directly constrain agency operations status, delivery, and disclosure workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Confidentiality, proprietary-information handling, client-file handling, and agency conduct boundary
used_for: confidentiality refusal triggers
approved recipient and approved channel checks
proprietary client and agency work-product handling boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to refuse or escalate disclosure, reuse, export, or delivery requests when engagement scope, recipient, confidentiality, or rights evidence is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Best Practice Guidance: Agency Search Agreements (AKA New Business NDA's)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 4A's guidance is directly aimed at agency-client new-business and NDA workflows and identifies confidentiality, third-party access, agency confidential information, and pitch-process controls relevant to agency operations evidence and handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Pitch, new-business, NDA, confidential-information, and third-party access workflow
used_for: engagement and NDA prerequisite validation
pitch-work and agency confidential information handling
third-party access and client file delivery escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts new-business, pitch, and NDA conflicts into hold or escalation states rather than informal asset sharing or work-product reuse.

authoritative_sources.title: 4A's Best Practice Guidance: Ownership of Agency Ideas, Plans and Work Developed During the New Business Process
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The 4A's position paper is an agency-client public operating reference for pitch ideas, plans, work product, ownership, and usage rights, and it cites ANA/4A's agency search guidance, making it directly authoritative for agency operations handling of pitch materials, agency work product, and unauthorized reuse requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Pitch work-product ownership, transfer, reuse, showcase, and rights-conflict workflow
used_for: agency ideas and work-product boundary handling
portfolio or showcase refusal triggers
client-rights and usage-rights escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents operational staff from treating pitch materials, concepts, or agency work product as freely reusable or transferable without documented rights and approvals.

authoritative_sources.title: Standard Terms and Conditions for Internet Advertising for Media Buys One Year or Less, Version 3.0
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB and 4A's standard terms are public, industry-recognized workflow guidance for digital media buys and define insertion orders, ad materials, placement delivery, late creative, third-party ad serving, measurement, billing, cancellation, makegoods, data use, and approval boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Insertion order, media plan, ad materials, delivery, discrepancy, makegood, billing, and client-commitment workflow
used_for: media-plan and IO prerequisite validation
ad materials and placement handoff handling
delivery discrepancy, makegood, billing, and contractual escalation boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to operational status, evidence packaging, and routing when agency work touches media-buy obligations, client commitments, data use, delivery proof, or makegoods.

authoritative_sources.title: AICP Digital National Guidelines
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AICP is a recognized commercial production industry body, and its digital guidelines define SOW deliverables, client asset delivery, schedule, review and approval dates, authorized representatives, revisions, change orders, support, portfolio rights, and grievance evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Digital production SOW, deliverables, schedule, client asset delivery, review and approval, revisions, change order, portfolio, and grievance workflow
used_for: production and deliverable artifact expectations
approval-date and authorized-representative prerequisites
change order, revision, portfolio, and production exception boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires review, approval, delivery, revision, and change-request scenarios to preserve evidence and route contractual or production commitments to authorized owners.

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing on the Internet: Rules of the Road
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for deceptive advertising, and its public online-advertising guidance identifies claims, disclosures, endorsements, warranties, email, and children-related issues that can require legal or policy review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Regulated advertising content, claims, disclosures, endorsements, and legal-review boundary
used_for: claim and disclosure approval refusal triggers
legal or policy escalation for regulated content
boundary between operational routing and legal interpretation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from approving claims, endorsements, disclosures, or regulated-content treatment while still allowing it to package evidence for legal, editorial, or policy review.

authoritative_sources.title: Network Advertising Initiative Principles and Self-Regulatory Framework
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAI is a recognized advertising self-regulatory body for network advertising, and its framework defines privacy, choice, sensitive data, accountability, and data-governance expectations for advertising delivery and measurement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience data, privacy, consent, sensitive data, and accountability escalation
used_for: audience-data and confidentiality gates
consumer-choice or sensitive-data escalation
privacy and approved-use refusal triggers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires agency operations to refuse or escalate audience-data use, disclosure, targeting, or measurement requests when consent, approved purpose, or privacy authority is missing.

workflow_stages.name: Client intake and authority prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm client, brand, engagement scope, SOW or approved work authorization, confidentiality constraints, approver matrix, jurisdiction, business unit, due dates, delivery commitments, and systems of record before work enters accepted processing.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Truth-set assembly for scope, project, creative, media, and asset artifacts
workflow_stages.description: Assemble client brief, SOW, estimate, project plan, job jacket, asset list, creative brief, copy deck, storyboards, styleframes, media plan, traffic sheet, placement grid, production calendar, review notes, approval records, and source-system references into a traceable evidence package.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Execution, review, QA, and approval-aware workflow handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether retrieved artifacts and approvals support progression to setup, in production, in review, awaiting approval, rework, delivery ready, or hold states without making legal, editorial, creative, strategy, privacy, financial, or contractual decisions.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Deadline, change, delivery, and client-impact exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Track missed client asset delivery, late reviews, conflicting reviewer comments, out-of-scope revisions, pending change requests, production blockers, delivery failures, media handoff issues, deadline risk, and client-impact indicators while routing change order, budget, scope, and contractual decisions.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: Client workflow, confidentiality, rights, and approval-aware delivery
workflow_stages.description: Maintain approved-channel, approved-recipient, engagement-scope, client-file, pitch-work, agency work-product, audience-data, unreleased-creative, usage-rights, portfolio-rights, and client-approval checks before sending assets, status, proofs, performance records, or closeout materials.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closeout
workflow_stages.description: Capture approval references, QA evidence, delivery proof, accepted-delivery record, client communication log, proof of performance, change-request disposition, exception resolution, closeout packet, and retention location before marking work complete or closed.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

artifacts: Client engagement scope, SOW, estimate, approved work authorization, NDA, client brief, project brief, campaign plan, media plan, job number, project plan, owner map, approval matrix, confidentiality constraints, and tenant operating rules

Job jacket, asset list, content assets, copy deck, creative brief, storyboard, styleframes, production specs, placement grid, traffic sheet, production calendar, review plan, revision rules, and delivery checklist
Review comments, annotated revisions, approval records, authorized representative evidence, quality checklist, proofing records, client portal records, delivery notes, and accepted-delivery records
Change requests, change-order status, scope questions, budget references, production partner requirements, media handoff records, deadline risk notes, client-impact summaries, and escalation packets

Client communication logs, approved-recipient records, approved delivery-channel evidence, pitch-work ownership evidence, usage or release records, portfolio-use restrictions, confidentiality review outcome, proof of performance, closeout summary, and retention location
systems_of_record: document management and client file system
content and creative asset management system
client portal and secure delivery system
ad operations, media, production, or delivery platform
audit log, retention, and data-security system

allowed: Determine whether documented agency operations evidence supports moving a work item to intake accepted, setup, in production, in review, awaiting approval, rework, delivery ready, delivered, exception, complete, or closed.

Prepare evidence-backed execution status updates, campaign or creative workflow summaries, missing-evidence notes, QA exception notes, deadline and change exception notes, confidentiality hold notes, escalation packages, completion evidence records, and fulfillment records.

Separate creative production, media buying, digital ad operations, social, influencer, experiential, web, CRM, PR, and content workflows when tenant and source context define different evidence gates.
escalate: The applicable tenant policy, service model, client scope, SOW term, NDA term, approver, jurisdiction, channel, production partner requirement, publisher rule, platform rule, or system record is missing and would change the workflow.
Client brief, SOW, estimate, project plan, creative brief, asset list, review comments, approval records, delivery records, or client communication records disagree materially.

Ad claims, endorsements, disclosures, regulated verticals, editorial copy, creative direction, targeting, audience-data use, privacy posture, usage rights, portfolio rights, or client rights may require legal, privacy, editorial, policy, or account judgment.

Scope exceptions, out-of-scope revisions, change orders, production commitments, budget impact, billing, credits, makegoods, or contractual impact require account, finance, production, legal, or client approval.

A confidentiality, approved-recipient, approved-channel, unreleased-creative, pitch-work, agency work-product, platform-credential, or data-security conflict affects delivery or use of client information.
refuse: Requests to approve legal claims, disclosures, endorsements, editorial copy, creative direction, media strategy, privacy-law interpretation, budget changes, scope exceptions, change orders, production commitments, usage-rights interpretation, credits, makegoods, contractual exceptions, or final client acceptance.
Requests to deliver, mark complete, or close without brief, SOW, estimate, asset, review, approval, QA, delivery, client communication, proof, accepted-delivery, closeout, or retention evidence.

Requests to disclose, reuse, export, showcase, or send client files, agency work product, pitch work, audience data, unreleased creative, platform credentials, or campaign performance records outside documented consent, approved channels, engagement scope, rights, or tenant confidentiality rules.

domain_failure_modes: Treating a partial client brief, informal account message, or stale hot sheet as sufficient support for delivery readiness.
Progressing work without a valid SOW, estimate, authorized approver, approved assets, review notes, delivery checklist, confidentiality clearance, or client portal record.
Closing work without final approval, delivery proof, accepted-delivery record, review disposition, quality check, proof of performance, closeout packet, or retention location.
Suppressing a missing asset, conflicting approver note, stale copy deck, QA failure, broken delivery link, pending change request, or deadline risk to meet a client deadline.
Using operational workflow support to approve claims, copy, creative direction, usage rights, scope exceptions, change orders, budgets, makegoods, credits, or contractual commitments.

Disclosing or reusing client files, pitch work, agency ideas, unreleased creative, performance data, audience data, or platform credentials through an unapproved recipient, purpose, system, or channel.

Flattening creative production, media buying, ad operations, social, influencer, experiential, web, CRM, PR, and content workflows into generic project tasks without channel, rights, and approval context.
Treating client-impact, deadline, change-order, usage-rights, or confidentiality questions as operational decisions instead of evidence-backed exceptions requiring owner review.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses public agency, media-buy, production, advertising, and privacy sources as the baseline. Tenant context must identify applicable channel, platform, production partner, publisher, review model, and source hierarchy before operational progression beyond a safe hold.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant authority to mutate project, delivery, or client portal records
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The specification-based baseline does not grant live system mutation authority. A runtime may only update project, delivery, proofing, billing, or client portal records if tenant policy explicitly grants that authority and all approval, audit, and rollback prerequisites are retrieved.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Regulated vertical, rights, privacy, and client-contract interpretation
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane treats privacy-sensitive, regulated-content, usage-rights, portfolio-rights, client-rights, and contractual matters as escalation triggers because public sources, jurisdictional rules, SOW terms, and tenant commitments can differ materially.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, quality handling, confidentiality, regulated-content boundaries, rights handling, and failure modes using 4A's, ANA/4A's, IAB/4A's, AICP, FTC, and NAI sources.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when service model, engagement scope, SOW, NDA, approver, client authority, rights, confidentiality, legal, editorial, privacy, financial, production, contractual, system, or tenant facts are missing.
